sanskrit: अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम् । परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम् ॥
hindiTranslation: मूर्खलोग मेरे सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंके महान् ईश्वररूप परमभावको न जानते हुए मुझे मनुष्यशरीरके आश्रित मानकर अर्थात् साधारण मनुष्य मानकर मेरी अवज्ञा करते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Fools despise Me when I dwell in a human form, not knowing My higher nature as the great Lord of all beings.
essence: Ordinary appearance can hide the highest reality.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People mock Krishna because they see only a human body and miss his higher nature as the lord of all beings.
simpleInsight: Respect can fail when we judge by appearance alone. Seeing more deeply changes everything.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Krishna keeps teaching Arjuna while the armies wait. He explains why some people dismiss him: they see only a human body and miss his vast nature as ruler of all beings. This verse answers that misunderstanding directly.
modernRelevance: You meet someone in a plain shirt and underestimate them before they speak. Or you dismiss a quiet teacher, a grieving parent, or a soft-spoken leader because the surface seems ordinary.
translationLiteral: अवजानन्ति (despise) / माम् (Me) / मूढाः (the deluded) / मानुषीम् (human) / तनुम् (form) / आश्रितम् (having taken refuge in) / परम् (higher) / भावम् (nature) / अजानन्तः (not knowing) / मम (My) / भूतमहेश्वरम् (great Lord of beings)
